titlestmt.titleproper: Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers3-12-4427

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-05-13 00:18:01 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers
did.abstract: Frank Harold Trevor Rhodes (1926-2020) was born in Warwickshire, England. The Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers document the early career of Frank Rhodes in the geologic sciences, paleontology, earth science, fossils, conodonts, and later work as an author dating from the 1940's to the 2010's.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to the permision of the family of Frank Rhodes.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Frank Harold Trevor Rhodes was born in Warwickshire, England, on Oct. 29, 1926. He earned his bachelor's, doctor of science and doctor of philosophy degrees in geology from the University of Birmingham, England. He taught at the University of Durham and the University of Wales, Swansea, where he also was dean of the faculty of science. He was a life fellow of Clare Hall, Cambridge, a visiting fellow of Trinity College, Oxford, and an honorary fellow of Robinson College, Cambridge.

In the United States, Rhodes taught at the University of Illinois and Ohio State University (as a Fulbright scholar and a National Science Foundation senior visiting research fellow, respectively) before joining the faculty at the University of Michigan in 1968 as a professor of geology. In 1971, he was named dean of Michigan's College of Literature, Science and the Arts and, from 1974-77, he served as Michigan's vice president for academic affairs.

He served as Cornell University President from 1977 to 1995, and maintained a role as a Presidential liaison in his emeritus capacity the rest of his life. He also continued to write and teach in the geological sciences and as a lifelong paleontologist and Darwin scholar.
He served on numerous boards including as trustee of King Abdullah University of Science and Technology.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers, #3-12-4427. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See Cornell Adult University records, #41-8-3726, for a CD of Rhodes trip to Japan, China, and Vietnam with CAU.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers document the early career of Frank Rhodes (1926-2020) in the geologic sciences, paleontology, earth science, fossils, conodonts, and later work as an author dating from the 1940's to the 2010's. In contains publications, drafts and research notes relating to books, speeches, slides, correspondence. Items pertaining to King Abdullah University of Science and Technology. Cornell Adult University teaching materials for courses he led. Items relating to his time at Birmingham, Swansea, Ohio, and Michigan.

controlaccess.subject: Geology.
Physical/Earth Sciences
Natural Resources Conservation and Protection
Conodonts
Higher Education
controlaccess.corpname: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains records from Rhodes' studies and career prior to his arrival at Cornell. Includes records of his undergraduate and graduate studies at the University of Birmingham, his teaching/research at the University of Durham, University of Illinois, the University of Wales, Swansea, and Ohio State University. This series also includes records pertaining to his work at the University of Michigan, first as professor of geology and then as Dean of the College of Literature, Science and the Arts.

scopecontent.p: Includes minority education information. Also includes advertisement for Cornell President position as well as some correspondence related to the position.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Correspondence in this subseries was consolidated by processing staff. Folders named after individuals by Rhodes were preserved, while loose correspondence was consolidated into chronological folders. In some cases, material extends beyond the scope of this series and into Rhodes' time at Cornell. As this material was stored with early material and in some cases interfiled with earlier correspondence, it was decided to store and arrange all of the correspondence together in this subseries.

did.unittitle: Oxford and Cambridge Schools Examination Board certificates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials documenting Rhodes' work at Cornell outside of his Presidential duties. Includes records of his inauguration and early years at Cornell, drafts and correspondence relating to his course lectures and teaching, research materials, and retirement. Also includes records documenting Cornell Adult University (CAU) trips led by Rhodes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by trip, as per Rhodes' original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes originals, photocopies, drafts, research, and correspondence relating to Rhodes' published articles and books.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by publication date. Co-authors and publisher are included in Scope and Content notes.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Rhodes' papers originally included two distinct series of articles. One series included publications and research/draft material while another contained photocopies and research/draft material. These series were consolidated and duplicates were weeded.

scopecontent.p: "LOTE" refers to the first edition of Language of the Earth. "LOTE II" refers to the second edition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily contains speeches given by Rhodes. Also includes speeches given by others, research and speech prep, and correspondence relating to speaking engagements.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by date speech was given by Rhodes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings of quotations removed from card files.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Rhodes' alphabetical subject listing was preserved.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings of quotations removed from card files and binders. Includes quotes described by Rhodes as serious, humor, and travel quotes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Rhodes' alphabetical subject listing was preserved.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains office files related to Rhodes' professional activities (primarily) following his retirement from Cornell. Includes research on higher education and various universities, donor correspondence, planning for the Cornell University Medical Center (CUMC), events (including class reunions), professorships and scholarships, and records of Rhodes' work in the American Philosophical Society (APS).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arrangement reflects Rhodes' original order of series and subseries.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted to permission of the curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence to and from Rhodes as he recovered from being struck by a car in February 2002.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides used in teaching, slides documenting Rhodes' trip to Australia, and slides documenting various Cornell Adult University trips.
